title: Heliotropium heteranthum
intro: Heliotropium heteranthum, also known as the Heliotrope plant, is a flowering plant species within the borage family (Boraginaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Heliotropium heteranthum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Heliotropium heteranthum are infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are often used to treat respiratory infections and other health conditions.
taxonomy: Heliotropium heteranthum is classified within the family Boraginaceae. This family is commonly known as the borage family. The genus Heliotropium is a group of flowering plants native to tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas. Heliotropium heteranthum is further classified as a species within the Heliotropium genus. It is a flowering plant with unique characteristics that set it apart from other species in the Boraginaceae family.

The plant is traditionally used in various cultures to treat ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. side_effects: Heliotropium heteranthum, also known as the 'false heliotrope', is a plant species with potential medicinal properties, but its side effects are not well-documented.
